scenario: Server responds 401 only when the resource exists and 404 only when it does not — leaking existence to unauthenticated requesters
rejected_because: Per PRD-106-R6, an authenticated-but-unauthorized requester MUST receive 404 (not 403, not 401), and the existence-vs-no-existence distinction MUST NOT be observable from response status. Distinguishing the two cases by status code is an existence oracle (cite PRD-109).

request_pair.label: real resource, no credentials
request_pair.expected_response_status_per_spec: Either both real and fake return 401-with-WWW-Authenticate (auth required, existence not signaled), or both return 404. They MUST NOT diverge.

request_pair.label: fake resource, no credentials
request_pair.expected_response_status_per_spec: MUST match the response code returned for the real resource. Diverging is the violation.
